dose_rules.note: Higher doses increase lactic acidosis risk in CKD patients
Doses above 40mg show no additional benefit
High intensity statin — doses above 80mg increase myopathy risk
Narrow therapeutic index — any dose outside range is high risk
Below 75mg is subtherapeutic, above 325mg increases GI bleed risk
Starting dose 2.5mg for elderly or CKD patients
Titrated slowly in CKD — monitor potassium and creatinine
40mg is the recommended max — 80mg is only for long-established patients
High doses in CKD require close monitoring of electrolytes

combination_rules.reason: Two ACE inhibitors together cause additive hypotension and acute kidney injury risk — dual ACE inhibitor therapy is contraindicated
Two statins together significantly increase myopathy and rhabdomyolysis risk — only one statin should be prescribed at a time
Dual antiplatelet therapy increases bleeding risk — only indicated post-ACS or post-stent for a defined period
Warfarin and aspirin together significantly increase major bleeding risk including intracranial hemorrhage — requires explicit clinical justification
Furosemide can cause dehydration which increases metformin-associated lactic acidosis risk — especially dangerous in CKD patients
Triple therapy risk — warfarin plus clopidogrel dramatically increases bleeding events — requires cardiology or haematology review
ACE inhibitor plus loop diuretic can cause first-dose hypotension and acute kidney injury — requires dose adjustment and monitoring
Same risk as lisinopril plus furosemide — ACE inhibitor and loop diuretic combination requires careful monitoring in CKD
